name: Yankee Pheasants Jubilee
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the birds and pat them dry.
2. Lightly salt the cavities of each bird.
3. Stuff each bird with quartered apples, onions, and celery leaves.
4. Cover each stuffed bird with slices of bacon.
5. Wrap the birds in foil and roast them in a shallow pan at 375°F for 1 1/2 hours.
6. During the last 1/2 hour of cooking, remove the foil to allow the birds to brown.
7. Remove the birds from the oven and set aside the tablespoon of drippings.
8. Discard the stuffing.
9. To make Jubilee sauce, combine the reserved drippings and cornstarch in a small saucepan over low heat.
10. Drain the cherries, reserving the syrup.
11. Combine the reserved syrup, white wine, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a separate bowl.
12. Stir this mixture into the cornstarch until it thickens and bubbles.
13. Add the cherries and margarine, stirring to combine.
14. Cook until the sauce thickens further and bubbles.
15. Carve the pheasants and serve with Jubilee sauce.
